text:
Ogyu (オーギュ, Ōgyu) is a game-only character from various Hunter × Hunter video games and a professional Card Hunter. He is typically one of the primary characters in the games he appears in.

Appearance

Ogyu has spiky blond colored hair and red eyes. He has a fair complexion and a thin build. He dresses in a blue motorcycle race suit with yellow segments and carries a stick with a figurine of himself attached to one end. 

Personality

Ogyu can be hot-tempered and impulsive toward people who cross him, but he can cool off if he's apologized to or shown to be in the wrong. He is also fairly confident in his abilities and typically prefers to work alone. 

Plot

Hunter's Genealogy

 Hunter × Hunter: Hunter's Genealogy 

Phantom of Greed Island

 Hunter × Hunter: Phantom of Greed Island 

Forbidden Treasures

 Hunter × Hunter: Forbidden Treasures 

Altar of Dragon Vein

 Note: Events occurring in the Altar of Dragon Vein do not constitute canon material. Furthermore, some events may slightly differ depending on the current player character. 

Ogyu appears in the post-game seeking the five Goddess Advent Cards. He manages to collect four out of five; however, Zegin Highline and his group steal them from him. He soon comes across Gon and Killua. Mistaking them for Zegin's henchmen, he attacks. After the battle, the boys explain themselves and bring Ogyu to the airship. He then joins them and seeks out the cards stolen from him and the last remaining card he has yet to achieve. 

When they manage to collect all of the cards, they realize that one of them was the one Ogyu did not manage to collect. They seek out the final card in Zegin's Mansion. This leads to a confrontation with the once-thought deceased Amane, who fulfills his goal of using Zegin's unconscious body for his own use. Amane and the manipulated corpse of Zegin fight the Hunter group and die in the aftermath. 

Now possessing all the five Goddess Advent Cards collected, Ogyu summons the Goddess from the cards and fulfills his wish. He requests to be able to travel the world and find the cards again, resulting in them scattering. When questioned, he explains that he prefers to find the cards alone. With that done, Ogyu goes off on another adventure but not before giving a ride to Romaria to the Midonite Government Environment Protection Bureau. 

The Stolen Aura Stone

 Hunter × Hunter: The Stolen Aura Stone 

Abilities & Powers

Ogyu is capable enough to have passed the notoriously difficult Hunter Exam. Furthermore, akin to a dowsing rod, he wields a stick that can be used to find aura-based items. He primarily uses it for card hunting or attacking in battle. When fighting, Ogyu is shown to be quite agile, leaping several feet away when attacking or dodging attacks. 

Nen

Due to being able to access Greed Island, Ogyu is capable of utilizing Nen. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 121 

Trivia
 
His name is an anagram of "Yu-Gi-Oh", a famous Japanese manga and its main character.
Much like the names of other Hunter × Hunter characters that appear written in an alternative way in some places (e.g. Hisoka's name as "Hyskoa"), his name is "Ohgyu" on a card that comes with Hunter × Hunter: Altar of Dragon Vein.
According to Hunter's Genealogy Game Guide Book, Ogyu was drawn by Yoshihiro Togashi for the game. 

Miscellaneous

Ogyu's Japanese voice actor also voices Rabid Dog from the 1999 anime adaptation.

Intertextuality and References

Ogyu's appearance (his hair mostly) resembles Yu-Gi-Oh!s main character, Yugi Muto.
